articles.title: China adds 10 US firms, including rare-earth miner, to export control list
articles.summary: Export ban follows move by Pentagon to blacklist major Chinese companies such as Alibaba and Baidu.
articles.titleZh: 中国将稀土矿商等10家美国企业列入出口管制名单
articles.summaryZh: 出口禁令是在五角大楼将阿里巴巴和百度等中国主要公司列入黑名单之后实施的。

contentParagraphs.en: Export ban follows move by Pentagon to blacklist major Chinese companies like Alibaba and Baidu.China has added 10 United States-based companies to its export control list and barred government procurement from nearly 50 US companies two weeks after the Pentagon blacklisted some of China’s best-known companies for their alleged ties to the Chinese military.China’s Ministry of Commerce announced the export order on Monday, barring Chinese companies from exporting “dual-use” items that can be used for civilian or military purposes to the US firms.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Japanese fans clean up stands after celebrating World Cup winlist 2 of 4Crimea halts fuel sales after Ukraine strikes cause shortageslist 3 of 4Salah scores against NZ as Egypt records historic win at World Cuplist 4 of 4Explosion at Qatar’s Ras Laffan LNG facility injures 54, leaves 18 missingend of listThe list of companies includes rare-earth mine operator MP Materials Corp, rare-earth magnet maker USA Rare Earths, and US defence contractors specialising in fields such as aerospace, drones, synthetic-aperture radar, and shipbuilding and repairs.Under the order, “foreign institutions and individuals worldwide are also prohibited from transferring or providing Chinese dual-use goods to them” while ongoing export transactions must be suspended immediately.The Commerce Ministry said the export ban had been issued to “safeguard national security and interests and fulfil international obligations such as non-proliferation”.China’s Ministry of Finance on Monday separately barred Chinese government procurement from 46 companies, including subsidiaries of major US defence contractors like Lockheed Martin, Boeing, General Atomics and General Dynamics. US-funded, locally registered companies, however, have been given an exemption by the ministry.Experts described Beijing’s orders as a retaliation, albeit a largely symbolic one, against the US after the Pentagon in early June added about 80 Chinese companies and their subsidiaries to its list of “Entities Identified as Chinese Military Companies Operating in the United States”.The designation means the Pentagon either believes the companies are owned or controlled by the Chinese military or they are “military-civil fusion contributors”, a term for commercial companies that contribute to China’s military development despite their civilian status.The updated list includes Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba Holdings, search engine giant Baidu and electric automaker BYD, some of China’s largest and best-known companies.While the order does not bar US companies from doing business with them, it does impact US defence contractors and their future supply chains.“We can interpret this as a tit-for-tat response, and that fits into China’s playbook any time we’ve seen escalation from the US side in terms of trade and investment tools,” said Nick Marro, global trade lead analyst at the Economist Intelligence Unit.China-based supply chain consultant Cameron Johnson said the Commerce Ministry’s order mirrors US semiconductor export controls designed to keep the most advanced chips out of Chinese hands.“They basically say it doesn’t matter where or who you are, you are bound by this regardless of circumstance,” said Johnson, who is also a senior partner at the Shanghai consultancy Tidal Wave Solutions. “Organisations or individuals in any country or region are prohibited from transferring dual-use materials that originated in China.”He said Beijing’s orders in practice may be hard to enforce and many of the companies named in those orders have already moved their supply chains out of China or begun to “de-risk” their operations there.Johnson said the wide scope of companies included in Washington’s and Beijing’s directives could be a sign of more to come and may signal a new front in the US-China trade war.“This is probably just the beginning of the back and forth,” he said. Last year, after returning to the White House for a second term, US President Donald Trump reignited the US-China trade war, leading Washington and Beijing to impose escalating rounds of tariffs on each other.Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping agreed to a trade truce in October, which was extended during a summit between the two leaders in Beijing in May.Despite promises to “enhance economic cooperation” during the meeting, observers like Singapore-based geopolitical analyst Steve Okun predicted the goodwill may be short-lived.“The US’s recent closure of chip export loopholes and China’s continuing addition to its export bans show the national security lane remains active in both capitals regardless of the diplomatic niceties at the recent Trump-Xi summit,” Okun told Al Jazeera.“There is no ‘truce’ in the US-China trade war. Expect further actions from both sides as well on export controls and investment restrictions,” he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 出口禁令是在五角大楼将阿里巴巴和百度等中国主要公司列入黑名单之后发布的。在五角大楼因涉嫌与中国军方有联系而将一些中国最著名的公司列入黑名单两周后，中国已将 10 家美国公司列入其出口管制名单，并禁止向近 50 家美国公司进行政府采购。中国商务部周一宣布了出口令，禁止中国公司向美国出口可用于民用或军用目的的“军民两用”物品推荐故事 4 项列表 4 项中的 1 项 日本球迷在庆祝世界杯胜利后清理看台 列表 4 项中的 2 项 乌克兰罢工导致短缺后，克里米亚停止燃料销售 列表 4 项中的 3 项 萨拉赫对新西兰进球，埃及在世界杯上取得历史性胜利 列表 4 项中的 4 项 卡塔尔拉斯拉凡液化天然气设施爆炸，造成 54 人受伤，留下 18 人失踪 名单中的公司包括稀土矿运营商 MP Materials Corp、稀土磁体制造商美国稀土公司，以及专门从事航空航天、无人机、合成孔径雷达、造船和维修等领域的美国国防承包商。根据该命令，“也禁止世界各地的外国机构和个人向其转让或提供中国的两用物品”，同时必须立即暂停正在进行的出口交易。商务部表示，发布出口禁令是为了“维护国家安全和利益，履行防扩散等国际义务”。中国财政部周一分别发布声明禁止中国政府向 46 家公司采购，其中包括洛克希德·马丁公司、波音公司、通用原子公司和通用动力公司等美国主要国防承包商的子公司。然而，美国出资的、在当地注册的公司却获得了国防部的豁免。专家们将北京的命令描述为对美国的报复，尽管在很大程度上是象征性的。六月初，五角大楼将约80家中国公司及其子公司添加到“被认定为在美国运营的中国军事公司的实体”名单中。这一指定意味着五角大楼要么认为这些公司由中国军方拥有或控制，要么认为它们是“军民结合”。聚变贡献者”，这是一个术语，指的是尽管处于民用地位但仍为中国军事发展做出贡献的商业公司。更新后的名单包括中国电子商务巨头阿里巴巴、搜索引擎巨头百度和电动汽车制造商比亚迪，这些都是中国最大、最知名的公司。虽然该命令并没有禁止美国公司与他们开展业务，但它确实影响了美国国防承包商及其未来的供应链。“我们可以将其解读为针锋相对的回应，这符合中国的策略经济学人智库全球贸易首席分析师尼克·马罗表示：“美国在贸易和投资工具方面的升级。”中国供应链顾问卡梅伦·约翰逊表示，商务部的命令反映了美国的半导体出口管制，旨在防止最先进的芯片落入中国手中。“他们基本上说，无论你在哪里或是谁，无论情况如何，你都将受到这一约束，”上海咨询公司 Tidal Wave Solutions 的高级合伙人约翰逊说。 “任何国家或地区的组织或个人都被禁止转移原产于中国的两用材料。”他表示，北京的命令实际上可能很难执行，这些命令中点名的许多公司已经将供应链移出中国，或开始“降低”其在中国的业务风险。约翰逊表示，华盛顿和北京的指令中包含的公司范围广泛，可能是未来更多的迹象，也可能标志着美中贸易战的新战线。“这可能只是一个开始来来回回，”他说。去年，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普在重返白宫连任后，重新点燃了美中贸易战，导致华盛顿和北京相互征收一轮又一轮的关税。特朗普和中国国家主席习近平于10月同意贸易休战，并在5月两国领导人在北京举行的峰会上延长了休战期。尽管会议期间承诺“加强经济合作”，但新加坡地缘政治分析师史蒂夫·奥肯等观察人士预测，善意可能会受到影响。奥肯对半岛电视台表示：“美国最近堵住了芯片出口漏洞，而中国不断增加出口禁令，这表明，无论最近的特习峰会上的外交礼节如何，两国首都的国家安全通道仍然活跃。”“美中贸易战没有‘休战’。预计双方在出口管制和投资限制方面也会采取进一步行动，”他说。

articles.title: AI models that can take down governments and business months away, rare Five Eyes statement warns
articles.summary:
Signal agencies in Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand and Canada sound alarm after Trump blocks foreign nationals from Anthropic’s Fable AI model
Powerful AI models capable of taking down governments and businesses are mere months away, cyber intelligence agencies for the Fiv
articles.titleZh: 罕见的“五眼联盟”声明警告称，人工智能模型可能会在几个月后摧毁政府和企业
articles.summaryZh:
在特朗普阻止外国人使用 Anthropic 的 Fable AI 模型后，澳大利亚、美国、英国、新西兰和加拿大的信号机构发出警报
五国网络情报机构距离推出能够摧毁政府和企业的强大人工智能模型仅几个月的时间

contentParagraphs.en: Powerful AI models capable of taking down governments and businesses are mere months away, cyber intelligence agencies for the Five Eyes have warned in a rare joint statement, urging leaders to “act now”.The surprising public intervention by signals agencies for Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand and Canada comes after the Trump administration earlier this month decided to block “foreign nationals” from using a much-hyped AI model built by tech company Anthropic, called Fable.The statement, issued late Monday night Sydney time, said while AI “would help us improve cyber defence over time, it also accelerates the speed, scale, and sophistication of cyber threats”.“Frontier AI models are anticipated to exceed current industry expectations, fundamentally transforming both offensive and defensive cyber capabilities. The timeline is not years, it is months,” the warning by Five Eyes’ agencies said.“In this environment, cyber resilience is integral to advancing business continuity, market confidence, and long-term value.”The cybersecurity agencies said the leaps in AI models showed the technology would lower barriers for bad actors and increase the speed and complexity of attacks.“A whole-of-organisation and whole-of-society response is required,” the statement continued.“Cyber risk can no longer be treated as a purely technical issue. This is a core business risk and leadership responsibility.”While no AI models or companies are by mentioned in the statement by name, many around the world have their eyes on Anthropic’s advanced tier of tools.One of the major tech company’s latest inventions is called Fable 5, a supposedly more community-friendly version of Mythos – a powerful AI model released earlier this year capable of detecting vulnerabilities in cyber systems that is only available to vetted organisations and companies because of concerns it could be exploited for bad.Both of Anthropic’s models were suspended for use by “foreign nationals” in June by the US government, which cited advice by national security authorities.Olivia Shen, an expert in national security and AI at the University of Sydney’s United States Studies Centre, said much of the world was focused on what happens next for Anthropic but there could be many more powerful AI models not far off on the horizon.“I think we have to anticipate that the next Mythos or the next Fable is just around the corner,” Shen said.“We can only see what’s been released, but there could be other models being developed by the likes of China, or other states and other actors and companies, that are just as advanced.”In March, the Albanese government signed Anthropic as the first company on to its national AI plan.The non-binding memorandum of understanding means companies agree to share details of AI progress with the government and “promote safety”.The government’s national plan promotes a light-touch approach on regulating the sector in a bid to capture economic and productivity benefits from the technology.
contentParagraphs.zh: 五眼联盟的网络情报机构在一份罕见的联合声明中警告说，距离政府和企业的强大人工智能模型只有几个月的时间，敦促领导人“立即采取行动”。澳大利亚、美国、英国、新西兰和加拿大的信号机构出人意料地进行了公开干预，此前特朗普政府本月早些时候决定阻止“外国人”使用由科技公司 Anthropic 开发的名为 Fable 的大肆宣传的人工智能模型。该声明于悉尼时间周一深夜发布，称人工智能“将随着时间的推移，它会帮助我们改善网络防御，同时也会加速网络威胁的速度、规模和复杂性。”五眼机构警告称，“前沿人工智能模型预计将超出当前行业预期，从根本上改变进攻性和防御性网络能力。时间不是几年，而是几个月。在这种环境下，网络弹性对于提高业务连续性、市场信心和长期价值至关重要。”网络安全机构表示，人工智能模型的飞跃表明，该技术将降低不良行为者和攻击者的障碍。声明继续说道：“需要全组织和全社会的响应。网络风险不能再被视为纯粹的技术问题。这是核心业务风险和领导责任。”虽然声明中没有提及人工智能模型或公司的名字，但世界各地的许多人都关注着 Anthropic 的高级工具。这家主要科技公司的最新发明之一被称为《神鬼寓言 5》，据称是Mythos 的社区友好版本——今年早些时候发布的一个功能强大的人工智能模型，能够检测网络系统中的漏洞，但由于担心它可能被利用而只适用于经过审查的组织和公司。美国政府引用了国家安全部门的建议，于 6 月暂停了 Anthropic 的两个模型供“外国人”使用。悉尼大学美国研究中心的国家安全和人工智能专家奥利维亚·沉 (Olivia Shen) 表示，世界大部分地区都在关注接下来会发生什么。 Anthropic 但可能还有许多更强大的人工智能模型即将出现。“我认为我们必须预见到下一个神话或下一个寓言即将到来，”沉说。“我们只能看到已经发布的内容，但中国、其他国家和其他参与者和公司可能正在开发其他同样先进的模型。”今年 3 月，阿尔巴尼亚政府签署了 Anthropic 作为其国家人工智能计划的第一家公司。谅解备忘录意味着公司同意与政府分享人工智能进展的细节并“促进安全”。政府的国家计划提倡采取宽松的方法来监管该行业，以从该技术中获取经济和生产力效益。

articles.title: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
articles.summary: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.
articles.titleZh: 现代美国经济的建筑师艾伦·格林斯潘去世，享年 100 岁
articles.summaryZh: 作为美联储主席，艾伦·格林斯潘成为世界上最引人注目的银行家。

contentParagraphs.en: Site search1 hour agoSam WoodhouseGetty ImagesFormer US Federal Reserve chair Alan Greenspan has died aged 100, his wife has said.NBC News correspondent Andrea Mitchell said in a statement reported by her employer that her husband had died from complications of Parkinson's Disease.Mitchell's statement said Greenspan was "a giant of a man who helped shape the US economy for decades under presidents of both parties, but was always honest in acknowledging his mistakes".For nearly 20 years, Alan Greenspan was charged with safeguarding the US economy and keeping the dollar sound.As chairman of the Federal Reserve from 1987-2006, a post described as the second most important after the presidency, he presided over the longest sustained period of US economic growth in a generation.Described as the "God in the machine" of American finance, Greenspan declined all requests for interviews during his time at the Fed.But the media and the money markets hung on his few public statements, and a sign in his office said simply, "the buck starts here".But critics argue that an over-reliance on easy credit fuelled the dot-com bubble of the late 1990s and caused the sub-prime mortgage crisis of 2008.Bettmann via Getty ImagesPresident Nixon gave him his entry into governmentAlan Greenspan was born in New York City on 6 March 1926. His mother, who worked in a furniture store, brought him up single-handed.Far from being a budding economist, the young Greenspan was a talented musician, who studied the clarinet at New York's renowned Julliard School of Music.He played in a band with Stan Getz, the legendary jazz saxophonist, before touring the country with the Henry Jerome Band. This peripatetic lifestyle gave him a valuable practical insight into the workings of US business.And while his fellow musicians spent their evenings smoking marijuana, Alan Greenspan busied himself by swotting up on economics and doing the band's accounts.At the age of 19, he enrolled as an economics student at New York University, where he became an apostle of the free market, and eventually found employment as an economic consultant and, later, as a member of the board at JP Morgan.Curbing inflationIn 1952, Greenspan met the right-wing novelist and social philosopher Ayn Rand, whose views were to have a profound influence on him.She called him "the undertaker" because of his liking for dark, sombre suits.But the young economist came to support her belief that society functions most efficiently when people actively pursue their own self-interests, to the exclusion of the interests of society as a whole.In an article he wrote in 1966, he declared "the welfare state" as "nothing more than a mechanism by which governments confiscate the wealth of the productive members of a society".The Chronicle Collection via Getty ImagesPresident Reagan announced Alan Greenspan as chairman of the Federal Reserve - America's central bank - in 1987Having successfully predicted the Eisenhower recession, Greenspan advised Richard Nixon during his successful presidential election campaign in 1968.He went on to become head of the Council of Economic Advisers.Greenspan later wrote that he found the president to be "sadly paranoid, misanthropic and cynical", but the economist's success at curbing inflation impressed Nixon's successors.Gerald Ford asked Greenspan to continue at the Council of Economic Advisers and - in the early 1980s - Ronald Reagan chose him to lead an inquiry into the reform of the America's state pension system.In August 1987, Reagan promoted him to chairman of the US Federal Reserve, and - for the next two decades - he became one of the most powerful men in the world.Golden eraHe was thrown in at the deep end. His astute handling of the October 1987 stock market crash, which saw more than 30% wiped off share prices, earned Greenspan many plaudits.His statement of confidence in the underlying economy calmed frayed nerves, and his facilitation of cheap credit helped keep the banks afloat. It was an approach used again and again, whenever the markets had a crisis. Later dubbed "quantitative easing", such upheavals included the 1980s savings and loan crisis, the first Gulf War, the Mexican peso crisis and - shortly after he had retired - the global credit crisis in 2008.Greenspan was renominated as chairman of the Federal Reserve by George H.W. Bush, although the president later complained that a sluggish economic recovery had put paid to his chances of re-election.Surprisingly, Bill Clinton - a Democratic Party president - also asked the driest of monetarists to stay on in post. But his decision was rewarded as, under Greenspan's direction, there followed a golden era of growth in the late 1990s.Greenspan later praised Clinton in his memoir for the president's "consistent, disciplined focus on long-term economic growth" - while complaining that some Republican administrations simply lost control of public spending.Archive Photos via Getty ImagesAlan Greenspan dated TV star Barbara Walters in the late 1970sAway from work, the rather grey-looking banker was a skilled and enthusiastic tennis player. An early marriage to a Canadian artist lasted less than a year, and Greenspan dated TV star Barbara Walters, before marrying NBC reporter Andrea Mitchell in 1997.The same year, the spectacular fall of the South East Asian "tiger economies" tested him again.By cutting US interest rates, he indicated his belief that the situation would recover and, in doing so, aided the world economy.Bubbles and crashesMuch the same happened when many dot-com companies, overpriced by investors, failed to live up to their hype and folded in March 2020.The market, said Greenspan, had exhibited "irrational exuberance".The Federal Reserve raised interest rates and then cut them rapidly after consumers vastly reduced their expenditure. But Greenspan was blamed for the low interest rate culture that had allowed the dot-com bubble to grow in the first place.The Nobel laureate
contentParagraphs.zh: 网站搜索1小时前萨姆·伍德豪斯盖蒂图片社前美联储主席艾伦·格林斯潘的妻子称已去世，享年100岁。NBC新闻记者安德里亚·米切尔在其雇主报道的一份声明中表示，她的丈夫死于帕金森病并发症。米切尔的声明称格林斯潘是“一位巨人，在两党总统领导下帮助塑造了美国经济数十年，但总是诚实地承认自己的错误”。近20年多年来，艾伦·格林斯潘肩负着维护美国经济和保持美元稳健的重任。1987年至2006年担任美联储主席（这一职位被称为仅次于总统的第二重要职务），他主持了一代人以来美国经济持续增长时间最长的时期。被称为美国金融界“机器中的上帝”的格林斯潘在美联储任职期间拒绝了所有采访请求。但媒体和货币市场却对他为数不多的公开声明表示关注，并暗示他将继续保持经济增长。他的办公室简单地说，“责任从这里开始”。但批评者认为，对宽松信贷的过度依赖助长了 1990 年代末的互联网泡沫，并引发了 2008 年的次贷危机。贝特曼（来自 Getty Images）尼克松总统让他进入政府。艾伦·格林斯潘于 1926 年 3 月 6 日出生于纽约市。他在一家家具店工作的母亲一手抚养他长大。年轻的格林斯潘从一名崭露头角的经济学家成长为一名才华横溢的音乐家，曾在纽约著名的茱莉亚音乐学院学习单簧管。他曾与传奇爵士萨克斯管演奏家斯坦·盖兹一起在乐队中演奏，随后随亨利·杰罗姆乐队在全国巡演。这种四处游历的生活方式让他对美国商业运作有了宝贵的实际见解。当他的音乐家同事们晚上吸食大麻时，艾伦·格林斯潘却忙于钻研经济学和做乐队的账目。19岁时，他就读于纽约大学经济学系，在那里他成为了自由市场的倡导者，并最终找到了一份经济顾问的工作，后来又成为 JP 的董事会成员。抑制通货膨胀 1952年，格林斯潘会见了右翼小说家和社会哲学家安·兰德，后者的观点对他产生了深远的影响。她称他为“殡葬者”，因为他喜欢深色、阴沉的西装。但这位年轻的经济学家开始支持她的信念，即当人们积极追求自身利益、排除整个社会的利益时，社会运转最有效。在他于1966年写的一篇文章中，他宣称“福利国家”被认为“只不过是政府没收社会生产性成员财富的一种机制”。 1987 年，里根总统宣布艾伦·格林斯潘出任美联储（美国中央银行）主席。 成功预测艾森豪威尔经济衰退后，格林斯潘在理查德·尼克松 1968 年成功的总统竞选期间为其提供建议。 他后来成为经济顾问委员会主席。 格林斯潘后来写道他发现总统“可悲地偏执、愤世嫉俗和愤世嫉俗”，但这位经济学家在抑制通货膨胀方面的成功给尼克松的继任者留下了深刻的印象。杰拉尔德·福特要求格林斯潘继续担任经济顾问委员会成员，而在 20 世纪 80 年代初，罗纳德·里根选择他领导对美国国家养老金制度改革的调查。1987 年 8 月，里根晋升他为美联储主席，并且 -在接下来的二十年里——他成为世界上最有权势的人之一。黄金时代他被扔进了深渊。 1987 年 10 月股市崩盘导致股价下跌超过 30%，他对股市崩盘的精明处理为格林斯潘赢得了许多赞誉。他对基本经济的信心声明安抚了紧张的情绪，他对廉价信贷的促进帮助银行维持运转。每当市场出现危机时，这种方法都会被反复使用。这些后来被称为“量化宽松”的剧变包括 20 世纪 80 年代的储蓄和贷款危机、第一次海湾战争、墨西哥比索危机以及他退休后不久的 2008 年全球信贷危机。格林斯潘被乔治·H·W·布什重新任命为美联储主席。布什总统后来抱怨经济复苏乏力，让他连任的机会付诸东流。令人惊讶的是，民主党总统比尔·克林顿也要求最干巴巴的货币主义者继续留任。但他的决定得到了回报，因为在格林斯潘的指导下，20世纪90年代末出现了经济增长的黄金时代。格林斯潘后来在回忆录中称赞克林顿总统“始终如一、有纪律地关注长期经济增长”，同时抱怨一些共和党政府根本失去了对公共支出的控制。档案照片来自盖蒂图片社艾伦·格林斯潘在1970年代末与电视明星芭芭拉·沃尔特斯约会。看上去头发花白的银行家是一位技艺高超、充满热情的网球运动员。格林斯潘早年与加拿大艺术家的婚姻维持了不到一年，格林斯潘与电视明星芭芭拉·沃尔特斯约会，然后于 1997 年与 NBC 记者安德里亚·米切尔结婚。同年，东南亚“四小龙经济体”的惊人衰退再次考验了他。通过削减美国利率，他表明了自己的信念，即形势将会复苏，并以此帮助世界经济。泡沫和崩溃同样发生在许多互联网公司因价格过高而被低估的情况下。投资者未能辜负他们的炒作，并于 2020 年 3 月倒闭。格林斯潘表示，市场表现出“非理性繁荣”。美联储提高利率，然后在消费者大幅减少支出后迅速降息。但格林斯潘被指责为低利率文化，正是这种文化首先导致了互联网泡沫的增长。 诺贝尔奖获得者

articles.title: Six ways to keep your home and yourself cool in hot weather
articles.summary: Six simple things you can do to help keep your house cool when temperatures rise.
articles.titleZh: 在炎热的天气里让你的家和你自己保持凉爽的六种方法
articles.summaryZh: 当气温升高时，您可以做六件简单的事情来帮助您的房子保持凉爽。

articles.title: Pakistan issues nationwide alert over fears of heavy rains, floods
articles.summary: Warning comes as country braces for likely fourth consecutive year of punishing monsoon, expected to arrive this month.
articles.titleZh: 巴基斯坦因担心暴雨和洪水而发布全国警报
articles.summaryZh: 警告发出之际，该国正准备迎接可能连续第四年的恶劣季风，预计将于本月到来。

contentParagraphs.en: Islamabad, Pakistan – Pakistan has entered what its disaster authority is calling a “critical” weather window.The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) on Sunday issued a nationwide alert, warning of thunderstorms, heavy rainfall, urban flooding, and an elevated risk of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) across the country’s northern regions over the next 12 to 24 hours.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4After the floods of 2025, can we keep 2026 above water?list 2 of 4Why Pakistani farmers are suing two German companies for deadly 2022 floodslist 3 of 4Pakistan’s climate change minister lashes out at US, ‘crisis of justice’list 4 of 4Mapping the scale of damage by the catastrophic Pakistan floodsend of listThe alert identified Hunza and Skardu areas in the mountainous Gilgit-Baltistan region in the north and the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province in the northwest among the most vulnerable areas to a possible climate disaster.Authorities also warned of flooding in capital Islamabad, and other urban areas, including Rawalpindi and its adjoining areas. Provincial and district administrations have been placed on high alert and directed to keep their drainage systems clear.The warning came as Pakistan braces for a likely fourth consecutive year of punishing monsoon, which is expected to arrive later this month.Melting glaciersLast year, monsoon rains in Pakistan killed more than 1,000 people, including 275 children, and displaced three million from their homes.But it was the historic floods in 2022 – mainly caused by melting glaciers and submerging nearly a third of the country – that put Pakistan on a global climate crisis watch.Pakistan contributes less than 1 percent of global emissions, yet remains among the five countries most affected by climate change.In Gilgit-Baltistan, temperatures this year reached a record 48.5 degrees Celsius (119.3 degrees Fahrenheit), breaking a previous high set in 1971. The heat has accelerated glacial melt, swelling and bursting lakes across the ecologically sensitive region.Pakistan is home to some 13,000 glaciers – the most in the world after the polar icecaps. And global warming is fast melting them.According to the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), melting glaciers across Pakistan’s Hindu Kush, Himalayas and Karakoram mountain ranges have formed more than 3,000 glacial lakes in Gilgit-Baltistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. Of those, 33 have been assessed as vulnerable to hazardous outbursts, with more than 7.1 million people living around them at risk.GLOFs release millions of cubic metres of water and debris within hours, destroying bridges, farms and entire communities downstream.In partnership with the UNDP, Pakistan in 2017 launched the scaling-up of the Glacial Lake Outburst Flood Risk Reduction project, known as GLOF-II, covering 24 valleys across 15 districts in Gilgit-Baltistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.The initiative focused on early warning systems, flood protection infrastructure, and community-based disaster preparedness.But Zakir Hussain, director general of the Gilgit-Baltistan Disaster Management Authority, told Al Jazeera that the scale of coverage under Pakistan’s early warning infrastructure is widely misunderstood.The GLOF-II project, he said, covered only 16 selected valleys, not Gilgit-Baltistan as a whole, and within those valleys, only a limited number of sites. In many of the areas hit hardest in 2025, including Ghizer, Diamer and parts of Hunza, no early warning system existed at all.“The problem there was the absence of coverage altogether,” Hussain told Al Jazeera.“The one exception is Shishper in Hunza valley. That is the single case where an early warning system was in place but did not generate a warning despite the glacier changing its behaviour. In the other instances, these are very different problems, and we should be clear about the distinction.”Funding gapThe 2022 floods remain the benchmark for how devastating climate disasters in Pakistan could turn. The floods killed nearly 1,700 people, displaced more than 30 million, caused $14.8bn in property damage, and wiped out $15.2bn from Pakistan’s gross domestic product.Pakistan hosted a donor conference in Geneva in January 2023, where about $11bn was pledged by various countries and international financial institutions for flood recovery. But according to the UN’s humanitarian coordination agency, OCHA, only about $4.5bn had been delivered by June 2025, largely for housing, transport and flood risk management projects.Hussain was direct about what that shortfall represents.“It is clear that the parties to the conference are not shouldering their responsibility when it comes to the transfer of funds, the transfer of technology, and the capacity building of countries suffering the consequences of carbon emissions by the developed world,” he said.What compounds the country’s vulnerability, Hussain added, is not only a lack of infrastructure, but also a lack of coordination between various institutions.“There is no single authoritative source of truth,” Hussain told Al Jazeera.“What one institution accepts, another does not, and that creates administrative hurdles and breakdowns in response. Integrating forecasting with response metrics is where the work needs to happen.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 巴基斯坦伊斯兰堡 - 巴基斯坦已进入其灾害主管部门所称的“关键”天气窗口。国家灾害管理局 (NDMA) 周日发布了全国警报，警告未来 12 至 24 小时内，该国北部地区将出现雷暴、强降雨、城市洪水，以及冰川湖溃决洪水 (GLOF) 的风险升高。推荐故事列表（共 4 项）列表 1（共 4 项）2025 年洪水过后，我们可以让 2026 年保持在水面上吗？list 2 of 4为什么巴基斯坦农民因 2022 年致命洪水起诉两家德国公司list 3 of 4巴基斯坦气候变化部长猛烈抨击美国，“正义危机”list 4 of 4绘制巴基斯坦灾难性洪水造成的损害规模发送列表警报确定了北部吉尔吉特-巴尔蒂斯坦山区的罕萨和斯卡杜地区以及北部西北部的开伯尔-普赫图赫瓦省是最容易遭受气候灾害影响的地区之一。当局还警告首都伊斯兰堡和其他城市地区（包括拉瓦尔品第及其毗邻地区）可能发生洪水。省和地区政府已处于高度戒备状态，并指示保持排水系统畅通。发出这一警告之际，巴基斯坦正准备迎接可能连续第四年的惩罚性季风，预计将于本月晚些时候到来。冰川融化去年，巴基斯坦的季风降雨导致 1,000 多人死亡，其中包括 275 名儿童，并使 300 万人流离失所。但这是 2022 年历史性的洪水 - 主要是由冰川融化造成的巴基斯坦近三分之一的国土被淹没，这让巴基斯坦成为全球气候危机的关注对象。巴基斯坦的排放量仅占全球排放量的不到 1%，但仍然是受气候变化影响最严重的五个国家之一。吉尔吉特-巴尔蒂斯坦今年的气温达到创纪录的 48.5 摄氏度（119.3 华氏度），打破了 1971 年创下的最高纪录。高温加速了生态敏感地区的冰川融化、膨胀和湖泊溃决。巴基斯坦拥有约 13,000 座冰川，是继极地冰盖之后世界上最多的冰川。全球变暖正在使它们迅速融化。根据联合国开发计划署 (UNDP) 的数据，巴基斯坦兴都库什山脉、喜马拉雅山脉和喀喇昆仑山脉的冰川融化，在吉尔吉特-巴尔蒂斯坦和开伯尔普赫图赫瓦省形成了 3000 多个冰川湖。其中，33 个被评估为容易遭受危险爆发，周围超过 710 万人面临风险。GLOF 在数小时内释放出数百万立方米的水和碎片，摧毁了桥梁、农场和下游的整个社区。巴基斯坦于 2017 年与联合国开发计划署合作，启动了扩大冰川湖溃决洪水风险降低项目（称为 GLOF-II），覆盖巴基斯坦 15 个地区的 24 个山谷。吉尔吉特-巴尔蒂斯坦和开伯尔-普赫图赫瓦。该倡议的重点是预警系统、防洪基础设施和以社区为基础的备灾。但吉尔吉特-巴尔蒂斯坦灾害管理局局长扎基尔·侯赛因告诉半岛电视台，巴基斯坦预警基础设施的覆盖范围被广泛误解。他说，GLOF-II项目只覆盖了16个选定的山谷，而不是吉尔吉特-巴尔蒂斯坦作为整个山谷中只有有限数量的遗址。在 2025 年受灾最严重的许多地区，包括吉泽尔、迪亚默和罕萨部分地区，根本不存在预警系统。侯赛因告诉半岛电视台：“问题是完全没有覆盖范围。”“一个例外是罕萨山谷的希什珀。这是唯一一个案例，预警系统已经就位，但尽管冰川改变了行为，但没有发出警告。在其他情况下，这些是非常不同的问题，我们应该清楚资金缺口 2022 年的洪水仍然是巴基斯坦毁灭性气候灾害如何转变的基准。洪水造成近 1,700 人死亡，超过 3000 万人流离失所，造成 148 亿美元的财产损失，并使巴基斯坦国内生产总值 (GDP) 损失 152 亿美元。 巴基斯坦于 2023 年 1 月在日内瓦主办了一次捐助者会议，各国和国际金融机构承诺提供约 110 亿美元用于洪水恢复。但根据联合国人道主义协调机构 OCHA 的数据，截至 2025 年 6 月，仅交付了约 45 亿美元，主要用于住房、交通和洪水风险管理项目。侯赛因直接说明了这一缺口所代表的含义。“很明显，在资金转移、技术转让以及遭受发达国家碳排放后果的国家的能力建设方面，会议各方没有承担起自己的责任，”他说。是什么加剧了该国的脆弱性，侯赛因补充说，这不仅是缺乏基础设施，而且还缺乏各个机构之间的协调。侯赛因告诉半岛电视台：“不存在单一的权威真相来源。一个机构接受的内容，另一个机构不接受，这会造成行政障碍和响应崩溃。将预测与响应指标相结合是需要开展工作的地方。”

articles.title: An AI proxy war could reshape Congress — before Congress reshapes AI
articles.summary: The massive spending and heated rhetoric in midterm races reflect the AI industry's political fault lines and competing visions of what the future should look like.
articles.titleZh: 一场人工智能代理人战争可能会重塑国会——在国会重塑人工智能之前
articles.summaryZh: 中期选举中的巨额支出和激烈的言论反映了人工智能行业的政治断层和对未来的相互竞争的愿景。

contentParagraphs.en: Scott Kwiatkowski takes part in a demonstration on May 23 at the Utah state Capitol in Salt Lake City to oppose construction of a data center in the state. Support and opposition to artificial intelligence are drawing tens of millions of dollars of spending during the midterm election cycle.
contentParagraphs.zh: 5 月 23 日，Scott Kwiatkowski 参加了在盐湖城犹他州议会大厦举行的示威活动，反对在该州建设数据中心。在中期选举周期中，对人工智能的支持和反对正在吸引数千万美元的支出。

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Politics newsletter, sent weekly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随时了解我们每周发送的政治时事通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: Groups tied to the artificial intelligence industry are flooding money into the midterms in hopes of shaping future AI regulation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 与人工智能行业相关的团体正在中期选举中投入大量资金，希望塑造未来的人工智能监管。

contentParagraphs.en: Around the country, groups associated with AI and tech are trying to influence elections from Senate races to local offices, even as Americans register increasing discomfort with the technology's ramifications for jobs, energy bills and society. AI-focused super PACs have already spent $43.3 million on congressional races this cycle, according to OpenSecrets, a nonprofit that tracks campaign spending.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在全国各地，与人工智能和科技相关的团体正试图影响从参议院竞选到地方办公室的选举，尽管美国人对这项技术对就业、能源法案和社会的影响越来越感到不安。根据跟踪竞选支出的非营利组织 OpenSecrets 的数据，专注于人工智能的超级政治行动委员会已经在本周期的国会竞选中花费了 4330 万美元。

contentParagraphs.en: The campaign blitz comes against a backdrop of bipartisan consensus that Congress needs to set more rules governing AI and the powerful companies developing it. Yet efforts to advance federal legislation have so far stalled.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这次竞选闪电战是在两党达成共识的背景下进行的，即国会需要制定更多规则来管理人工智能和开发人工智能的强大公司。然而，推进联邦立法的努力迄今为止仍处于停滞状态。

contentParagraphs.en: The massive spending and heated rhetoric reveal a great deal about the contours of Silicon Valley's political fault lines and competing visions of what the future should look like.
contentParagraphs.zh: 巨额支出和激烈言辞充分揭示了硅谷政治断层线的轮廓以及对未来的相互竞争的愿景。

contentParagraphs.en: "This type of spending really helps shape who is at the table and what perspectives they are bringing into those conversations when new legislation is crafted," said Michael Beckel, director of money in politics reform at Issue One, a bipartisan nonprofit that seeks to reduce the influence of money in politics.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这种类型的支出确实有助于塑造谁在谈判桌上，以及在制定新立法时他们在对话中引入什么观点，”问题一号政治改革资金主任迈克尔·贝克尔说，该组织是一个两党非营利组织，致力于减少金钱在政治中的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's rewriting the playbook for how industries are trying to exert their influence in Washington and in states across the country," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说：“这正在改写各行业如何在华盛顿和全国各州发挥影响力的剧本。”

contentParagraphs.en: The proxy war in Central Park
contentParagraphs.zh: 中央公园的代理人战争

contentParagraphs.en: An early test of how this strategy could pay off will come Tuesday in a congressional primary in New York City that has drawn more than $15 million in AI-backed spending both for and against Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee who is pushing to more strictly rein in the industry.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周二在纽约市举行的国会初选将对该策略如何发挥作用进行早期测试，该初选已吸引了超过 1500 万美元的人工智能支持支出，无论是支持还是反对 Alex Bores，他是 Palantir 前员工，正在推动更严格地控​​制该行业。

contentParagraphs.en: Bores, 35, is a New York state assemblyman who co-sponsored the state's Responsible AI Safety and Education Act, legislation that requires AI companies to report safety incidents and publish information on their safeguards.
contentParagraphs.zh: 35 岁的博雷斯是纽约州议员，他是该州《负责任的人工智能安全和教育法案》的共同发起人，该法案要求人工智能公司报告安全事件并发布有关其保障措施的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: In October 2025, he entered the Democratic primary race to replace retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler in New York's 12th Congressional District. It spans the heart of Manhattan, north from 14th Street to the top of Central Park, and has the highest per-capita income in the country.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2025 年 10 月，他进入纽约第 12 国会选区的民主党初选，接替即将退休的众议员杰里·纳德勒 (Jerry Nadler)。它横跨曼哈顿市中心，北起第14街，一直到中央公园山顶，是全美人均收入最高的地区。

contentParagraphs.en: The primary race — which will likely determine who replaces Nadler in the Democratic stronghold — has become a major battle in the proxy war over federal AI regulation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 初选可能决定谁将取代纳德勒在民主党大本营中的地位，这已成为联邦人工智能监管代理人战争中的一场重大战役。

contentParagraphs.en: After Bores entered the race, super PACs tied to investors in ChatGPT maker OpenAI unleashed a torrent of spending aimed at torpedoing his campaign. An early anti-Bores ad argued laws like New York's RAISE Act would create a "chaotic patchwork of state rules that would crush innovation."
contentParagraphs.zh: 博雷斯参加竞选后，与 ChatGPT 制造商 OpenAI 的投资者有联系的超级政治行动委员会发起了大量支出，旨在破坏他的竞选活动。早期的反博雷斯广告称，像纽约的《RAISE Act》这样的法律会造成“混乱的州规则拼凑，从而压制创新”。

contentParagraphs.en: Groups linked to OpenAI and Anthropic have collectively spent more than $15 million on pro- and anti-Bores messaging, according to Federal Election Commission filings.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据联邦选举委员会的文件，与 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 相关的团体总共花费了超过 1500 万美元用于支持和反对 Bores 的信息传递。

contentParagraphs.en: Think Big PAC/Screenshot by NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: Think Big PAC/NPR 截图

contentParagraphs.en: Think Big PAC/Screenshot by NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: Think Big PAC/NPR 截图

contentParagraphs.en: Rival Anthropic, an AI company founded by OpenAI defectors that has called for more regulation, is backing super PACs countering the OpenAI-aligned groups' assault on Bores with millions of dollars of their own.
contentParagraphs.zh: Rival Anthropic 是一家由 OpenAI 叛逃者创立的人工智能公司，呼吁加强监管，该公司正在支持超级 PAC，以数百万美元的资金来反击 OpenAI 联盟组织对 Bores 的攻击。

contentParagraphs.en: Groups linked to the two companies have collectively spent more than $15 million on pro- and anti-Bores messaging, according to Federal Election Commission filings.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据联邦选举委员会的文件，与这两家公司有联系的团体在支持和反对鲍里斯的信息上总共花费了超过 1500 万美元。

contentParagraphs.en: The torrent of ads, mailers and texts appears to have primarily served to raise Bores' profile in a crowded field ahead of the June 23 primary.
contentParagraphs.zh: 广告、邮件和短信的洪流似乎主要是为了在 6 月 23 日初选之前在拥挤的领域中提高博雷斯的形象。

contentParagraphs.en: Corporate rivals fund opposing super PACs
contentParagraphs.zh: 企业竞争对手为反对的超级政治行动委员会提供资金

contentParagraphs.en: The Bores contest is the most visible arena in which the AI sector's intramural rivalries are spilling into politics.
contentParagraphs.zh: Bores 竞赛是人工智能领域内部竞争蔓延至政治领域最明显的舞台。

contentParagraphs.en: In their quest to win the AI race, OpenAI and Anthropic compete for everything from funding and staff to customers. They're both planning massive initial public offerings later this year. And they are locked in an ideological feud over how AI should be built, commercialized and governed, which shapes their respective views on the role of regulation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为了赢得人工智能竞赛，OpenAI 和 Anthropic 展开了从资金、员工到客户等方方面面的竞争。他们都计划在今年晚些时候进行大规模的首次公开募股。他们在人工智能应该如何构建、商业化和治理方面陷入了意识形态分歧，这形成了他们对监管作用的各自看法。

contentParagraphs.en: On one side of the political fight is Leading the Future, mainly funded by venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz, which is an OpenAI investor, and OpenAI's president and co-founder, Greg Brockman. Its stated mission is to "oppose policies that stifle innovation, enable China to gain global AI superiority, or make it harder to bring AI's benefits into the world, and those who support that agenda." It argues for a national approach to setting AI standards and safeguards.
contentParagraphs.zh: 政治斗争的一边是Leading the Future，主要由风险投资公司Andreessen Horowitz（OpenAI 的投资者）和OpenAI 的总裁兼联合创始人Greg Brockman 资助。其宣称的使命是“反对那些扼杀创新、使中国获得全球人工智能优势或使人工智能的好处更难惠及世界的政策，以及那些支持这一议程的人。”它主张采用国家方法来制定人工智能标准和保障措施。

contentParagraphs.en: OpenAI president Greg Brockman has helped fund the Leading the Future super PAC, which is opposed to stricter regulations on AI companies.
contentParagraphs.zh: OpenAI 总裁格雷格·布罗克曼 (Greg Brockman) 资助了“引领未来”超级政治行动委员会，该组织反对对人工智能公司实施更严格的监管。

contentParagraphs.en: Leading the Future has raised more than $75 million. It's already spent $23.5 million on dozens of races from Texas and Georgia to Illinois and Montana through a network of super PACs, including Think Big and American Mission, according to OpenSecrets' tally of federal filings. The group has also funded a PAC supporting Republican Byron Donalds' campaign for governor of Florida, a hotspot in the state-level fight over AI regulation.
contentParagraphs.zh: “Leading Future”已筹集超过 7500 万美元。根据 OpenSecrets 的联邦文件统计，该公司已经通过 Think Big 和 American Mission 等超级政治行动委员会网络，在从德克萨斯州、佐治亚州到伊利诺伊州和蒙大拿州的数十场比赛中花费了 2350 万美元。该组织还资助了一个 PAC，支持共和党人拜伦·唐纳德 (Byron Donalds) 竞选佛罗里达州州长，这是州级人工智能监管斗争的热点。

contentParagraphs.en: On the other side is Public First, positioned in direct opposition to Leading the Future. In February, Anthropic announced it was contributing $20 million to a related nonprofit, Public First Action, that "opposes federal efforts to freeze state progress without adequate federal safeguards." Anthropic said the company didn't want to "sit on the sidelines" while AI policies are developed and warned that "v
contentParagraphs.zh: 另一边是“公共第一”，其定位与“引领未来”直接对立。今年 2 月，Anthropic 宣布将向相关非营利组织 Public First Action 捐款 2000 万美元，该组织“反对联邦在没有充分联邦保障措施的情况下冻结各州进展的努力”。 Anthropic 表示，该公司不想在制定人工智能政策时“袖手旁观”，并警告说“v

articles.title: Leading Lebanese conservationist dies after an Israeli airstrike on her home
articles.summary: Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.
articles.titleZh: 黎巴嫩著名自然资源保护主义者在以色列空袭她的家后死亡
articles.summaryZh: 莫娜·哈利勒 (Mona Khalil) 的海滨住宅于两周前遭到以色列空袭，于周五去世。她因在黎巴嫩南部发起了一场保护海龟筑巢地的保护运动而受到赞誉。

contentParagraphs.en: Mona Khalil, a Lebanese ecologist activist, looks at a turtle in southern Lebanon in August 2002.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2002年8月，黎巴嫩生态活动家莫娜·哈利勒（ Mona Khalil ）在黎巴嫩南部观察了一只海龟。

contentParagraphs.en: BEIRUT — Lebanese conservationist Mona Khalil was first introduced to a green sea turtle as she was drinking a beer on the beach and a female turtle laying eggs threw sand over her, according to a volunteer with the decades-long effort she began to save the endangered animals.
contentParagraphs.zh: 贝鲁特—黎巴嫩自然资源保护主义者莫娜·哈利勒（ Mona Khalil ）在海滩上喝啤酒时首次被介绍给一只绿色海龟，一只产卵的雌性海龟向她投掷沙子，据一位志愿者说，她花了数十年的努力才开始拯救濒临灭绝的动物。

contentParagraphs.en: Khalil, 76, died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon that protected sea turtle nesting grounds and southern Lebanon's Mediterranean coast.
contentParagraphs.zh: 两周前， 76岁的哈利勒在以色列空袭袭击她的海滨家园后于周五去世。她在黎巴嫩南部发起了一场保护运动，保护海龟筑巢地和黎巴嫩南部的地中海沿岸。

contentParagraphs.en: Her housekeeper, who is Ethiopian, sustained less-severe injuries in the attack, Khalil's relatives said. The two women were the only occupants of what was known as "the Orange House," just steps from the al-Mansouri beach near the city of Tyre.
contentParagraphs.zh: 哈利勒的亲属说，她的管家是埃塞俄比亚人，在袭击中受伤不太严重。这两位女士是所谓的“橙色房子”的唯一居住者，距离提尔市附近的al-Mansouri海滩仅几步之遥。

contentParagraphs.en: The Israeli military said last week in response to an NPR query that it had no indication it had hit the house but was reviewing its records. It did not respond to a query about when the review might be completed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 以色列军方上周在回应NPR的询问时表示，没有迹象表明它袭击了这所房子，但正在审查其记录。它没有回复有关何时可以完成审核的询问。

contentParagraphs.en: Israel has invaded southern Lebanon and is attacking what it says are Iran-backed Hezbollah fighters and infrastructure. The Lebanese health ministry says more than 4,000 people have been killed since the war began on March 2, including at least 600 women and children. Israel says 35 soldiers and a military contractor, along with two civilians, have been killed in Hezbollah attacks.
contentParagraphs.zh: 以色列入侵了黎巴嫩南部，并正在攻击其所谓的伊朗支持的真主党战斗人员和基础设施。黎巴嫩卫生部表示，自3月2日战争开始以来，已有4000多人丧生，其中包括至少600名妇女和儿童。以色列说， 35名士兵和一名军事承包商以及两名平民在真主党的袭击中丧生。

contentParagraphs.en: Fadia Joumaa, a former volunteer who took over the turtle conservation effort, says Khalil had vowed to stay in her home during the fighting, believing she was safe because she was a civilian and there were no nearby targets.
contentParagraphs.zh: 接管海龟保护工作的前志愿者Fadia Joumaa说， Khalil发誓在战斗期间留在家中，认为她是安全的，因为她是平民，附近没有目标。

contentParagraphs.en: Khalil trained a generation of volunteers in ecological conservation, protecting the Mediterranean coastline and the endangered sea turtles that travel hundreds of miles to return to the same beaches where they were hatched to lay their eggs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 哈利勒培训了一代生态保护志愿者，保护地中海海岸线和濒临灭绝的海龟，这些海龟跋涉数百英里返回孵化产卵的海滩。

contentParagraphs.en: Human encroachment, trash in the ocean and animal predators that eat the eggs and hatchlings mean newly hatched turtles have only about a 1 in 1,000 chance of surviving to adulthood.
contentParagraphs.zh: 人类的侵占、海洋中的垃圾以及吃蛋和幼龟的动物捕食者意味着新孵化的海龟只有大约千分之一的机会存活到成年。

contentParagraphs.en: The volunteers find clutches of eggs laid at night in late summer, protecting them with wire mesh. They then help the tiny turtles reach the water once hatched.
contentParagraphs.zh: 志愿者们找到了夏末晚上产下的蛋，用丝网保护它们。然后，它们帮助小海龟在孵化后到达水面。

contentParagraphs.en: Rami Khachab, 32, a herpetologist originally from al-Mansouri, said he started volunteering in high school — going out with Khalil before dawn to walk the beaches looking for turtle nests.
contentParagraphs.zh: 32岁的爬行动物学家拉米·哈查布（ Rami Khachab ）最初来自曼苏里（ al-Mansouri ） ，他说他开始在高中做志愿者—在黎明前与哈利勒一起在海滩上寻找海龟巢。

contentParagraphs.en: He says that after her introduction to the turtles during her evening drink on the beach roughly 25 years ago, Khalil reached out to European turtle protection organizations to learn everything she could about the creatures. She began monitoring nests, collecting data and working to keep the green sea and loggerhead turtle nests safe.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说，大约25年前，哈利勒在海滩上晚上喝酒时介绍海龟，之后，她联系了欧洲海龟保护组织，了解有关这些生物的一切信息。她开始监测巢穴，收集数据，并努力保护绿海和海龟巢穴的安全。

contentParagraphs.en: "Through the Orange House, she inspired generations of Lebanese to value and protect their natural heritage and coastal ecosystems. Her work made her one of Lebanon's most respected voices for marine conservation and biodiversity protection," said the environmental group Green Southerners.
contentParagraphs.zh: “通过Orange House ，她激励了几代黎巴嫩人珍惜和保护他们的自然遗产和沿海生态系统。她的工作使她成为黎巴嫩最受尊敬的海洋保护和生物多样性保护的声音之一，”环保组织绿色南方人说。

contentParagraphs.en: It called for those responsible for the killings of Khalil and other civilians to be held accountable.
contentParagraphs.zh: 它呼吁追究那些对杀害哈利勒和其他平民负有责任的人的责任。

contentParagraphs.en: Joumaa, a Lebanese journalist, first met Khalil intending to do a story about her.
contentParagraphs.zh: 黎巴嫩记者Joumaa第一次见到哈利勒，打算做一个关于她的故事。

contentParagraphs.en: "You have to sweat and work hard the way I do before writing a single word," she says Khalil told her. Joumaa ended up not writing the story, but instead spent years volunteering with her before Khalil retired in 2020.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她说，哈利勒告诉她： “在写一个单词之前，你必须像我一样汗流浃背，努力工作。” Joumaa最终没有写这个故事，而是在Khalil于2020年退休之前花了数年时间与她一起做志愿者。

contentParagraphs.en: By that time, Khalil had turned the Orange House into an ecotourism guesthouse, an educational space for children and sea turtle observation point.
contentParagraphs.zh: 到那时，哈利勒已将橙屋变成了生态旅游宾馆、儿童教育空间和海龟观察点。

contentParagraphs.en: Joumaa says Khalil's work opposing the privatization of beaches and building along the southern coast eventually transformed the turtle nesting grounds into an officially recognized community-based conservation area.
contentParagraphs.zh: Joumaa说，哈利勒反对海滩私有化和南部海岸建筑的工作最终将海龟筑巢地变成了一个官方认可的社区保护区。

contentParagraphs.en: But these conservation efforts, including a successful campaign to ban the use of dynamite in fishing, didn't always go smoothly. "Mona was a fighter. She did not like diplomacy. There were times when they shot at her house," Joumaa says, referring to local opponents.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但这些保护工作，包括成功禁止在捕鱼中使用炸药的运动，并不总是顺利进行。“莫娜是个斗士。她不喜欢外交。有时他们会向她的房子开枪，” Joumaa说，他指的是当地的对手。

contentParagraphs.en: "She always told me: Defend the beach, defend the turtles, defend your country."
contentParagraphs.zh: “她总是告诉我：保卫海滩，保卫海龟，保卫你的国家。”

contentParagraphs.en: Jawad Rizkhallah contributed reporting from Beirut.
contentParagraphs.zh: Jawad Rizkhallah自贝鲁特对本文有报道贡献。

articles.title: Far-right de la Espriella elected Colombia president: What’s next?
articles.summary: Election results show Trump-endorsed millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins by razor-thin margin.
articles.titleZh: 极右翼德拉埃斯普里埃拉当选哥伦比亚总统：下一步是什么？
articles.summaryZh: 选举结果显示，特朗普支持的百万富翁阿韦拉多·德拉埃斯普里埃拉以微弱优势获胜。

contentParagraphs.en: Far-right millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella, who was endorsed by United States President Donald Trump, has won Colombia’s presidential election, according to preliminary results.De la Espriella, 47, achieved a narrow victory against his left-wing challenger, Senator Ivan Cepeda, who was backed by outgoing leftist President Gustavo Petro.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4From outsider to president? Inside de la Espriella’s bid to lead Colombialist 2 of 4Continuity or change? What to know about Colombia’s run-off electionlist 3 of 4Colombia presidential candidate De la Espriella celebrates preliminary leadlist 4 of 4Far-right lawyer De La Espriella wins Colombia’s tight presidential raceend of listDe la Espriella’s victory in Sunday’s run-off will set the stage for an overhaul of the economic, foreign and domestic policies pursued under Petro, who tried to address Colombia’s inequality, conducted peace talks with armed groups and severed ties with Israel over its genocidal war on Gaza. Petro also banned exports of coal to Israel and joined South Africa’s genocide case against Israel at the International Court of Justice.De la Espriella has promised to restore ties with Israel and move Colombia’s embassy to Jerusalem, following along the lines of Argentinian President Javier Milei.Here is more about what the election result means and what is next:What was the election result?De la Espriella won 49.66 percent of the vote while Cepeda trailed him by about 250,000 votes at 48.7 percent support, according to the national registrar’s tally of just under 100 percent of the ballots.Cepeda, 63, had pledged to maintain the policies of Petro, a former rebel and Colombia’s first leftist president. Those policies included state pension payments for the poor, union-backed labour reforms, a moratorium on new oil projects and continued peace talks with armed groups.More than 26.3 million of the 41.4 million eligible voters cast ballots in Sunday’s election.De la Espriella emerged with a small advantage over Cepeda in the first round of voting on May 31, earning 43 percent of the vote compared with the senator’s 40 percent.Cepeda told his supporters at an event in Bogota that he would await a final ballot-by-ballot check of the initial count, saying his campaign is challenging the results from about 33,000 polling stations, nearly a quarter of the total of 122,000.“We are open to dialogue. We are willing to reach agreements as long as they are respectful, genuine and reflected in political actions that benefit the nation and preserve the historical progress we have already achieved,” Cepeda said.A final verified count, overseen by notaries and judges, is required by Colombian law and was nearly finished late on Sunday. It was not clear yet whether the final results matched the initial count.Security was a key concern for many de la Espriella voters, especially in regions where extortion and drug trafficking have risen recently.But many Cepeda supporters feared de la Espriella’s hardline stance against armed groups could return the country to a more active conflict. For more than 60 years, the Latin American nation has been a battleground for leftist rebels, drug cartels and criminal gangs founded by former right-wing paramilitaries.“De la Espriella’s victory marks a dramatic ideological reversal. Just four years after Colombia elected its first ever left-wing president, the country has swung hard to the right, joining a regional wave of outsider, strongman politics alongside Milei, [El Salvador President Nayib] Bukele and Trump,” Annette Idler, an associate professor in global security at the University of Oxford’s Blavatnik School of Government, told Al Jazeera.“But the result also lays bare just how deeply polarised Colombia is. He won by less than 1 percentage point, blank and null votes alone outnumbered his margin of victory and more than half the country did not support him. This is not a mandate for radical change. It is a portrait of a nation almost exactly divided.”Cepeda told his supporters at an event in Bogota that he would await a final ballot-by-ballot check of the initial count, saying his campaign is challenging the results from about 33,000 polling stations [Sergio Acero/Reuters]Who is the new president of Colombia?If the final results are consistent with the preliminary results, de la Espriella, a lawyer and businessman with no prior political experience, will become the new president of Colombia.He has presented himself as a businessman, but an investigation by the online newspaper La Silla ‌Vacia found ⁠that many of his businesses have been dissolved, are in debt and have lost money. His law firm is his most profitable endeavour, it reported. He is also a citizen of the US and Italy and has homes in multiple countries.De la Espriella has blamed Petro for Colombia’s economic and security troubles, including a rise in violence linked to armed groups, and has pledged to end talks with rebels and criminal groups. He has also promised to boost the oil and gas sector, lower taxes and reduce the size of the state by up to 40 percent. He has said, however, that he will preserve Petro’s 23 percent increase in the minimum wage along with other popular social measures.De la Espriella also contested the election on the promise that if he won, he would launch intense military operations for 90 days against armed groups in the country. Inspired by Bukele’s aggressive policies against drug gangs, he has pledged to build mega-prisons.“I will govern for all Colombians, for those who voted for me and for those who chose the other candidate,” de la Espriella told a crowd of supporters in the coastal city of Barranquilla, promising to respect all citizens’ rights.“De la Espriella benefitted from widespread disillusionment with the Petro government, which leaves office with serious unresolved crises in security, public finances and healthcare,” Idler said.Idler explained that this public sent
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据初步结果，得到美国总统唐纳德·特朗普支持的极右翼百万富翁阿韦拉多·德拉埃斯普里埃拉赢得了哥伦比亚总统选举。47岁的德拉埃斯普里埃拉以微弱优势击败了他的左翼挑战者参议员伊万·塞佩达，后者得到了即将卸任的左翼总统古斯塔沃·佩特罗的支持。推荐故事列表 4 条列表 1 of 4 从局外人到总统？德拉埃斯普里埃拉 (de la Espriella) 力争带领哥伦比亚队 2 of 4 继续还是改变？了解哥伦比亚第二轮选举名单 4 名中的 3 名哥伦比亚总统候选人德拉埃斯普里埃拉庆祝初步领先名单 4 名中的 4 名极右翼律师德拉埃斯普里埃拉赢得了哥伦比亚激烈的总统竞选名单结束德拉埃斯普里埃拉在周日决选中的胜利将为佩特罗领导下的经济、外交和国内政策的彻底改革奠定基础，佩特罗试图解决哥伦比亚的不平等问题，与武装团体进行和平谈判并断绝关系与以色列就其对加沙的种族灭绝战争进行了对话。石油公司还禁止向以色列出口煤炭，并在国际法院加入南非针对以色列的种族灭绝案。德拉埃斯普里埃拉承诺恢复与以色列的关系，并将哥伦比亚大使馆迁至耶路撒冷，效仿阿根廷总统哈维尔·米莱的做法。以下是有关选举结果意味着什么以及下一步的更多信息：选举结果是什么？德拉埃斯普里埃拉赢得了 49.66% 的选票，而塞佩达落后他约根据国家登记处近乎 100% 的选票统计，获得 25 万张选票，支持率为 48.7%。 63 岁的塞佩达曾承诺维持前叛军、哥伦比亚第一位左翼总统佩特罗的政策。这些政策包括为穷人支付国家养老金、工会支持的劳工改革、暂停新的石油项目以及继续与武装团体进行和平谈判。4140万合格选民中有超过2630万人在周日的选举中投票。在5月31日的第一轮投票中，德拉埃斯普里埃拉以微弱优势领先塞佩达，获得了43%的选票，而参议员的得票率为40%。塞佩达告诉他的支持者在波哥大举行的一次活动中表示，他将等待对初步计票结果进行最后的逐票检查，并表示他的竞选活动正在对约 33,000 个投票站的结果提出质疑，占总数 122,000 个投票站的近四分之一。塞佩达说：“我们愿意进行对话。我们愿意达成协议，只要这些协议是尊重的、真诚的，并反映在造福国家和维护我们已经取得的历史进步的政治行动中。”由公证人和法官监督，这是哥伦比亚法律的要求，周日晚些时候几乎完成。目前尚不清楚最终结果是否与最初的计票结果相符。安全是许多德拉埃斯普里埃拉选民关心的一个主要问题，特别是在最近敲诈勒索和贩毒活动增多的地区。但许多塞佩达支持者担心德拉埃斯普里埃拉对武装团体的强硬立场可能会让该国陷入更加激烈的冲突。 60多年来，这个拉丁美洲国家一直是左翼叛乱分子、贩毒集团和前右翼准军事人员建立的犯罪团伙的战场。“德拉埃斯普里埃拉的胜利标志着意识形态的戏剧性逆转。哥伦比亚选出第一位左翼总统仅四年后，该国就猛烈向右转，与米莱、[萨尔瓦多总统纳伊布]布克莱和牛津大学布拉瓦特尼克政府学院全球安全副教授安妮特·伊德勒告诉半岛电视台。“但结果也暴露了哥伦比亚的两极分化程度有多么严重，仅空白票和无效票就超过了他的胜利优势，而且超过一半的国家不支持他。这实际上是一个几乎完全分裂的国家的写照。”在波哥大，他将等待初步计票的最终逐票检查，并称他的竞选活动正在对约33,000个投票站的结果提出质疑 [Sergio Acero/路透社]谁是哥伦比亚新总统？如果最终结果与初步结果一致，没有从政经验的律师和商人德拉埃斯普里埃拉将成为哥伦比亚新总统。他以商人的身份自居，但网络报纸《La Silla》的调查显示，他将成为哥伦比亚新总统。瓦西亚发现，他的许多企业已经解散，负债累累，亏损。据报道，他的律师事务所是他最赚钱的事业。他也是美国和意大利公民，在多个国家拥有家园。德拉埃斯普里埃拉将哥伦比亚的经济和安全问题归咎于佩特罗，其中包括与武装团体有关的暴力事件增加，并承诺结束与叛乱分子和犯罪团体的谈判。他还承诺振兴石油和天然气行业、降低税收并将国家规模缩小多达 40%。不过，他表示，他将保留 Petro 提高 23% 的最低工资以及其他受欢迎的社会措施。德拉埃斯普里埃拉还在竞选中承诺，如果他获胜，他将对该国的武装团体发动为期 90 天的密集军事行动。受布克莱针对贩毒团伙的激进政策的启发，他承诺建造大型监狱。“我将为所有哥伦比亚人、为那些投票给我的人和那些选择其他候选人的人执政，”德拉埃斯普里埃拉在沿海城市巴兰基亚对一群支持者说道，并承诺尊重所有公民的权利。伊德勒说：“在安全、公共财政和医疗保健方面。”伊德勒解释说，这位公众发送了

articles.title: It's not just about nudity warns actress - the complex reality of images and online abuse
articles.summary: Tech companies and authorities are failing women by focusing on nudity rather than consent, says a report by Chayn.
articles.titleZh: 女演员警告说，这不仅仅是裸体问题 - 图像和网络虐待的复杂现实
articles.summaryZh: 查恩的一份报告称，科技公司和当局关注裸体而不是同意，从而辜负了女性的期望。

contentParagraphs.en: Site searchIt's not just about nudity warns actress - the complex reality of images and online abuse14 hours agoMegha MohanandFay Nurse,BBC World ServiceBBCPakistani actress Ayesha Omar says she lost work when images of her in a swimsuit and shorts were shared onlineSocial media companies and authorities are failing women by focusing on nudity rather than consent when dealing with image-based abuse, according to a new report by gender justice organisation Chayn.Its criticisms are backed by Pakistani actress Ayesha Omar, whose experiences, along with those of other women, are described in the findings.One of the women at the centre of the report, whose name has been changed to Mahnoor to protect her privacy, explains that the images that changed her life were not nude. They were not sexually explicit. They showed a woman exposing her bare shoulders and wearing Western clothing.The 32-year-old from Pakistan told BBC Global Women that she returned to her childhood home when her marriage broke down. She hoped for comfort and support from her family, but instead, she and her young daughter were met with iciness.It's been over a year and her father and brothers still have not spoken to her. Colleagues at work who she has known for years will not look her in the eye.Mahnoor had expected a difficult divorce. It had never been an easy marriage. She says her husband, to whom she was married in an arranged match, was both verbally and physically abusive throughout their relationship. But it was the exposure of her private world that cost her the most.Like many young women, Mahnoor had saved lots of pictures of herself on her phone. She had taken photos of her everyday life - a nice dinner, a selfie when the lighting was particularly flattering. Many were years old. One was of her smiling after a new haircut. Another showed her on an overseas exchange programme with friends. Others were ordinary selfies, lying in bed, wearing a vest, with her eyes closed to show off her eyeliner. None had ever been shared publicly. She rarely posted photos on social media, mindful of the conservative culture of her community in Pakistan.According to Mahnoor, who is a university lecturer, her former husband gained access to her WhatsApp account and private images before distributing them to male relatives, colleagues and acquaintances.Mahnoor says he also cropped images of her with a group of friends, to make it appear that she was standing with a single man, insinuating that they were having an affair.The photographs, she says, were used to portray her as "a woman of bad character", an accusation that, in many communities, can carry life-altering and sometimes fatal consequences.Emco Conference 2026Report author, Hera Hussain, warns an "image does not have to be nude for it to be harmful"With her friends and family, as well as colleagues, barely engaging with her, Mahnoor says she has lost her social standing and the once powerful position she held in her community."I lost my voice," she told the BBC. "I no longer felt visible."My family once respected me, my brothers respected me. Having your voice respected by your parents is such a great thing," she says. They used to ask for her advice, but that is no longer the case.Mahnoor's ex-husband has now remarried.The report highlighting Mahnoor's story is by Chayn, a global non-profit organisation that examines gender-based violence. Chayn argues that image-based abuse is routinely misunderstood by both authorities and technology companies because they continue to define harm primarily through nudity.Titled Explicit Harms of Non-Explicit Images, the report argues that for many women, a fully clothed image can have consequences every bit as devastating as an intimate photograph within their wider, and often conservative, communities."The image does not have to be nude for it to be harmful," says Hera Hussain, report author and founder of Chayn. "Sometimes it can be as harmful, even if not a single body part is bare."We want to reframe the conversation around image-based abuse away from nudity and towards consent."For years, public conversations about image-based abuse have focused on so-called revenge pornography, deepfake nudes and sexually explicit content. But Chayn's research suggests that this framework misses how shame, reputation and social control operate in many communities.A photograph that appears entirely ordinary to one person may carry severe consequences for another. A video clip showing a woman dancing at a wedding. A photograph of a woman at the beach. A selfie shared without permission.Cultural sensitivitiesThe report argues that harm is often determined not by what the image contains, but by why it is shared, who receives it and what consequences follow.Chayn conducted 64 interviews between July 2025 and February 2026 and participants spanned every major region of Pakistan as well as diaspora communities in the UK, Canada, Germany, Malaysia, the UAE and Kuwait. The research catalogues the kinds of images women feared seeing shared: hair visible without a headscarf, Western or fitted clothing, a photograph taken beside a man who is not a relative, a screenshot of a fabricated conversation, or an image generated by AI from a single photo of someone's face. None contain nudity. All can be made to tell a damaging story.For Ayesha Omar, the argument is not theoretical. The actress, who has worked in Pakistan's film and television industry for more than 20 years, says her own images were stolen and circulated long before social media made such exposure commonplace. Photographs taken on a holiday over a decade ago in Thailand with a female friend, on a beach, where she wore a one-piece swimsuit and shorts, were taken from a laptop without her knowledge and posted online."It was very damaging for my career," Ayesha says. "I lost ad campaigns. I lost some work stuff." She takes a pause before adding: "Because in my culture, you have to conform to a particular
contentParagraphs.zh: 网站搜索警告女演员，这不仅仅是裸体问题 - 图像和网络虐待的复杂现实 14小时前 梅格哈·莫哈和费伊·护士，BBC世界服务台 BBCP巴基斯坦女演员阿耶莎·奥马尔表示，当她穿着泳衣和短裤的照片在网上分享时，她失去了工作 根据性别正义组织 Chayn 的一份新报告，社交媒体公司和当局在处理基于图像的虐待时关注裸体而不是同意，从而让女性失望。由巴基斯坦女演员阿伊莎·奥马尔 (Ayesha Omar) 撰写，调查结果中描述了她的经历以及其他女性的经历。报告中心的一名女性（为了保护隐私，她的名字已更改为 Mahnoor）解释说，改变她生活的照片并不是裸体。他们没有露骨的性行为。照片中，一名女子裸露肩膀，穿着西式服装。 这位来自巴基斯坦的 32 岁女子告诉 BBC 全球女性，她在婚姻破裂后回到了儿时的家。她希望得到家人的安慰和支持，但她和小女儿却遭到了冷遇。一年多了，父亲和兄弟仍然没有和她说过话。她认识多年的同事不会直视她的眼睛。马努尔原本预计离婚会很艰难。这从来都不是一桩轻松的婚姻。她说，她的丈夫是在一场包办婚姻中结婚的，在他们的关系中，她的丈夫一直在言语和身体上虐待他。但让她付出最大代价的是她的私人世界的曝光。像许多年轻女性一样，马努尔在手机上保存了大量自己的照片。她拍了自己日常生活的照片——一顿丰盛的晚餐，一张在灯光特别迷人的时候的自拍照。许多人已经年老了。其中一张是她换了新发型后微笑的照片。另一张照片展示了她与朋友参加的海外交流项目。其他都是普通的自拍，躺在床上，穿着背心，闭着眼睛秀眼线。没有任何一个被公开分享过。她很少在社交媒体上发布照片，考虑到巴基斯坦社区的保守文化。大学讲师马赫努尔表示，她的前夫在将其分发给男性亲戚、同事和熟人之前，获得了她的 WhatsApp 帐户和私人图像。马赫努尔说，他还裁剪了她和一群朋友的照片，让她看起来像是和一个单身男人站在一起，暗示他们有外遇。她说，这些照片是用来描绘马努尔称她为“性格恶劣的女性”，这一指控在许多社区可能会带来改变生活、有时甚至是致命的后果。2026 年 Emco 会议报告的作者赫拉·侯赛因 (Hera Hussain) 警告说，“图像不一定是裸体的，因为它是有害的”。由于她的朋友、家人以及同事几乎不与她接触，马努尔说她已经失去了社会地位，以及她在社区中曾经拥有的强大地位。“我失去了声音，”她告诉媒体英国广播公司。 “我不再觉得自己是可见的。”我的家人曾经尊重我，我的兄弟也尊重我。让父母尊重你的声音是一件很棒的事情，”她说。他们过去常常征求她的建议，但现在情况不再是这样了。马努尔的前夫现在已经再婚了。重点介绍马努尔故事的报告是由 Chayn 撰写的，Chayn 是一家研究性别暴力的全球非营利组织。Chayn 认为，基于图像的虐待经常被当局和科技公司误解，因为他们仍然主要通过以下方式来定义伤害：这份题为《非露骨图片的露骨危害》的报告认为，对于许多女性来说，在更广泛且通常是保守的社区中，全副武装的照片可能会产生与亲密照片一样的破坏性后果。报告作者兼 Chayn 创始人赫拉·侯赛因 (Hera Hussain) 表示，“有时，即使身体没有一个部位是裸露的，它也可能同样有害。”基于图像的虐待远离裸体，转向同意。”多年来，关于基于图像的虐待的公众讨论一直集中在所谓的报复色情、深度伪造裸体和露骨的性内容上。但查恩的研究表明，这个框架忽略了羞耻、声誉和社会控制在许多社区中的运作方式。一张对一个人来说完全普通的照片可能会给另一个人带来严重的后果。一段视频剪辑显示一名女子在婚礼上跳舞。一张女人在海滩上的照片。未经许可分享的自拍照。文化敏感性该报告认为，伤害往往不是由图像所包含的内容决定的，而是由分享的原因、接收者以及随之而来的后果决定的。Chayn 在 2025 年 7 月至 2026 年 2 月期间进行了 64 次采访，参与者遍布巴基斯坦的每个主要地区以及英国、加拿大、德国、马来西亚、阿联酋和科威特的侨民社区。该研究对女性害怕看到的分享图像进行了分类：不戴头巾的头发、西式或合身的衣服、在非亲戚的男人身边拍摄的照片、捏造的对话截图，或者人工智能根据某人脸部的单张照片生成的图像。没有一个包含裸体。一切都可以讲述一个具有破坏性的故事。对于阿耶莎·奥马尔来说，这个论点不是理论上的。这位在巴基斯坦影视行业工作了 20 多年的女演员表示，早在社交媒体让此类曝光变得司空见惯之前，她自己的照片就被窃取和传播。十多年前，与一位女性朋友在泰国度假时在海滩上拍摄的照片，当时她穿着连体泳衣和短裤，这些照片在她不知情的情况下从笔记本电脑上被盗并发布到网上。“这对我的职业生涯造成了很大的损害，”阿耶莎说。 “我失去了广告活动。我失去了一些工作资料。”她停顿了一下，然后补充道：“因为在我的文化中，你必须遵守特定的规则。

articles.title: EasyJet says US bidder trying to buy it 'on the cheap' as it rejects £4.7bn offer
articles.summary: The airline describes the latest bid approach from Castlelake as "highly opportunistic".
articles.titleZh: 易捷航空表示，美国竞购者拒绝 47 亿英镑的报价，试图“低价”收购该公司
articles.summaryZh: 该航空公司将卡斯尔莱克的最新投标方式描述为“高度机会主义”。

contentParagraphs.en: EasyJet says US bidder trying to buy it 'on the cheap' as it rejects £4.7bn offer3 hours agoFaarea Masud,Business reporterandTheo Leggett,Business correspondentGetty ImagesEasyJet has rejected a takeover offer worth £4.74bn from US investment firm Castlelake, accusing it of trying to buy the airline "on the cheap".The carrier's comments came after Castlelake said it had made three takeover approaches to the airline this month, all of which had been rejected.The US firm has now made details of its latest offer public to allow shareholders to assess the proposal. Under stock market rules, Castlelake has until Friday to make a firm offer or walk away.EasyJet is one of Europe's largest airlines, and last year carried more than 90 million passengers. It operates across 38 countries on more than 1,200 routes.The carrier repeated its accusation that Castlelake's offer was "highly opportunistic", arguing that its share price had been "temporarily depressed" partly due to the impact of Iran war on the travel sector.Under Castlelake's latest offer, the airline's shareholders would receive 625p per share, a 24% premium to last Friday's closing price.The US firm- which already owns a stake of about 2.14% in EasyJet through the funds it manages - said its latest bid "offers compelling value" to the carrier's shareholders."Following the rejection of three proposals by the EasyJet Board, and given its unwillingness to engage meaningfully, Castlelake is announcing this Third Proposal to enable EasyJet shareholders to consider its merits," Castlelake said."Castlelake's ambition is to support EasyJet as a stronger, more resilient European airline under European control, respecting EasyJet's valuable airline assets and continuing to sustain its network."European Union regulations stipulate that EasyJet must be majority-owned by EU citizens. Castlelake said it had proposed an ownership structure which was a "deliverable solution to ensure compliance with all applicable regulatory requirements."This will involve Castlelake going into partnership with two EU nationals, businessmen Peter Bellew and Mark Breen. They will own an EU-based company which will have majority control of the airline.Peter Bellew is a former chief operating officer of EasyJet, who has also held the same role at Ryanair. He left EasyJet in 2022, after a turbulent period in which staff shortages led to major disruption, including a large number of cancellations.Mark Breen runs an aerospace consultancy, and has previously held senior roles at a number of airlines, including several in the Middle East.EasyJet said the proposed ownership structure was "opaque" and did not present any basis for assessing the deliverability of the takeover plan.
contentParagraphs.zh: 易捷航空表示，美国竞购者试图“低价”收购该航空公司，因为它拒绝了 47 亿英镑的报价。3 小时前，商业记者法雷亚·马苏德 (Faarea Masud) 和商业记者西奥·莱格特 (Theo Leggett) 盖蒂图片社 易捷航空 (EasyJet) 拒绝了美国投资公司 Castlelake 价值 47.4 亿英镑的收购要约，指责该公司试图“低价”收购该航空公司。 在 Castlelake 表示本月已向该航空公司提出三项收购意向后，该航空公司发表了上述评论，所有这些意向均已在这家美国公司现已公开其最新要约的详细信息，以便股东评估该提议。根据股票市场规则，Castlelake 必须在周五之前提出确定报价，否则就走开。EasyJet 是欧洲最大的航空公司之一，去年载客量超过 9000 万人次。该公司在 38 个国家/地区运营超过 1,200 条航线。该航空公司再次指责 Castlelake 的报价是“高度机会主义”，并称其股价“暂时低迷”，部分原因是伊朗战争对旅游业的影响。根据 Castlelake 的最新报价，该航空公司的股东将获得每股 625 便士，较上周五收盘价溢价 24%。 Castlelake 表示，通过其管理的基金持有 EasyJet 约 2.14% 的股份，其最新出价为该航空公司的股东“提供了令人信服的价值”。“在 EasyJet 董事会拒绝三项提案后，鉴于其不愿有意义地参与，Castlelake 宣布了第三项提案，以使 EasyJet 股东能够考虑其优点。”Castlelake 表示。“Castlelake 的目标是支持 EasyJet 成为欧洲控制下的一家更强大、更具弹性的欧洲航空公司，尊重易捷航空宝贵的航空资产并持续维持其网络。”欧盟法规规定易捷航空必须由欧盟公民持有多数股权。 Castlelake 表示，它提出了一种所有权结构，这是“确保遵守所有适用监管要求的可交付解决方案”。这将涉及 Castlelake 与两名欧盟公民、商人 Peter Bellew 和 Mark Breen 建立合作伙伴关系。他们将拥有一家总部位于欧盟的公司，该公司将拥有该航空公司的多数控制权。Peter Bellew 是易捷航空 (EasyJet) 的前首席运营官，他也在瑞安航空 (Ryanair) 担任过同样的职务。他于 2022 年离开易捷航空，当时经历了一段动荡时期，人员短缺导致重大混乱，包括大量航班取消。马克·布林经营着一家航空航天咨询公司，此前曾在多家航空公司担任高级职务，其中包括中东的几家航空公司。易捷航空表示，拟议的所有权结构“不透明”，没有为评估收购计划的交付能力提供任何依据。
